message:
feat(metrics): tick 164 handle_connection_with_cx + start_with_cx spawn routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Fifteenth post-storage tick. metrics.rs had `start_with_cx` already
shipped but its per-connection spawn at L565 still routed through
the legacy `handle_connection`. Closing that half-migration.

Structure:
- handle_connection (legacy, preserved) — reads 8KB, factored out
  the response body into `handle_connection_impl(socket, prefix,
  collector, buf: Vec<u8>)` to avoid duplicating the HTTP
  formatting + write + shutdown logic.
- handle_connection_with_cx (new) — pre-flight cx.checkpoint +
  post-read cx.checkpoint, then routes through the same
  `handle_connection_impl`. A cx cancel arriving between the
  accept in start_with_cx and the 8KB read short-circuits the
  handler with `crate::Error::Runtime("metrics handle_connection
  cancelled: ...")`.

Refactor note: the extracted `handle_connection_impl` takes
`socket: TcpStream` by value (not `&mut TcpStream`). That preserves
the asupersync `TcpStream::shutdown(Shutdown::Both)` inherent
method — passing by &mut resolves to the `AsyncWriteExt::shutdown()`
trait method with a different argcount and breaks compilation.

Call-site migration in `start_with_cx` (L519):
- L565: `handle_connection(socket, &prefix, collector)` →
  `handle_connection_with_cx(&conn_cx, socket, &prefix, collector)`
  with `let conn_cx = task_cx.clone();` added before the spawn
  so each per-connection task carries its own cx.

Legacy `start` spawn at L484 still routes through
`handle_connection`, preserving the ambient-cx path.

Verified via fork bypass: 74/74 metrics:: + policy_metrics:: tests
pass, including the existing
`metrics_server_wait_with_precancelled_cx_returns_quickly` +
`metrics_server_start_with_cx_happy_path_serves_request`
regression gates which transitively cover the new cx chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
